blockers.description: Freeze block bbox fix + compute_dims 3-tuple refactor not yet UAT-confirmed in live Nuke
blockers.workaround: none — requires live Nuke with freeze blocks and normal trees

human_actions_pending.action: Run live Nuke UAT: (1) freeze block with members left AND right of root — verify no overlap; (2) normal non-freeze layout — verify no regression from refactor; (3) right-only freeze extension — verify no regression.
human_actions_pending.context: compute_dims now returns (W, H, root_x_offset) 3-tuple. For normal nodes root_x_offset=0 so behavior is identical. For freeze block leaves root_x_offset=left_overhang. place_subtree reads root_x_offset from child_dims[i][2] — no freeze_block_sizes parameter needed. All 335 tests pass.

decisions.decision: compute_dims returns 3-tuple (W, H, root_x_offset) instead of 2-tuple
decisions.rationale: Eliminates freeze_block_sizes side channel from place_subtree. Freeze block bounding box flows through same dims data model as all subtrees. root_x_offset=0 for normal nodes — zero behavioral change.

decisions.decision: place_subtree centers ROOT NODE (inp.screenWidth()) above consumer, not allocation band width
decisions.rationale: Centering by allocation width causes cascading leftward drift in deep chains. Centering root node matches old behavior. alloc_left derived as root_x - root_x_offset for band accounting.

decisions.decision: freeze_block_sizes parameter removed from place_subtree, kept on compute_dims
decisions.rationale: place_subtree reads root_x_offset from child_dims[i][2]. compute_dims still needs freeze_block_sizes as input to compute freeze block leaf dimensions.

next_action: Ask user to run UAT in live Nuke — test freeze blocks AND normal layout to confirm refactor didn't change behavior. If passes, commit and push.
context_notes: This session: reviewed layout algorithm architecture, identified that compute_dims (W,H) 2-tuple had no way to express left overhang, and that the prior fix (freeze_block_sizes side channel + _left_ovh/_centered_alloc_left closures) was patches on patches. Refactored to 3-tuple (W, H, root_x_offset) model where root_x_offset is embedded in the dims return value. place_subtree uses child_dims[i][2] uniformly. For centering, root is placed at _center_x(inp.screenWidth(), x, node_w), then alloc_left = root_x - child_dims[i][2]. Non-freeze nodes have root_x_offset=0 so all behavior is identical to before the freeze feature existed.
